claim: AlphaFold solved protein 3D structure prediction, which is only one part of drug discovery; the full pipeline also needs compound design that binds correctly without side effects
actionable: When building AI systems for drug discovery, treat AlphaFold as a starting point; build additional AI layers for compound generation and binding affinity prediction
source_title: Demis Hassabis: We're Three Quarters of the Way to AGI

claim: AI-driven drug discovery could reduce discovery time from 10 years to months or weeks by moving 99% of exploration to in-silico simulation and reserving wet lab work for final validation
actionable: Prioritize building in-silico simulation capabilities for drug candidate screening; use wet lab work only for validation of computationally pre-screened candidates
source_title: Demis Hassabis: We're Three Quarters of the Way to AGI

claim: Information is the fundamental building block of the universe equivalent to matter and energy; understanding systems as information processing is more powerful than viewing them as matter/energy systems
actionable: When studying complex systems (biology, physics, computation), frame analysis through the lens of information processing and organization rather than just material composition
source_title: Demis Hassabis: We're Three Quarters of the Way to AGI

claim: AI systems for simulating complex domains (weather, biology) could unlock new sciences by enabling repeated controlled experiments on systems where real-world control studies are impossible
actionable: Build high-fidelity generative simulators for domains like economics and social systems where you cannot run repeated controlled experiments in reality
source_title: Demis Hassabis: We're Three Quarters of the Way to AGI

claim: Classical Turing machines can model systems previously thought to require quantum computing, as demonstrated by AlphaFold solving protein folding without quantum effects
actionable: When facing complex systems, test classical approaches before assuming quantum computing is necessary; many phenomena may be classically modelable with better algorithms
source_title: Demis Hassabis: We're Three Quarters of the Way to AGI

claim: AGI timeline estimate of 2030 remains consistent with field progress; this represents the fulfillment of a 20-year prediction made in 2010 when DeepMind was founded
actionable: Use 2030 as the planning horizon for strategic decisions dependent on AGI capabilities; align research roadmaps to bridge current gaps by that timeline
source_title: Demis Hassabis: We're Three Quarters of the Way to AGI

claim: Building AGI as a tool first before addressing questions of agency and consciousness is the responsible approach, allowing us to use the tool to understand consciousness better
actionable: When building advanced AI systems, focus first on capabilities and reliability; defer philosophical questions about consciousness and agency to when we have tool-level AGI
source_title: Demis Hassabis: We're Three Quarters of the Way to AGI
